messages.content: List the weirdest Eurovision acts of all time

1. Lordi (Finland, 2006) - a heavy metal band dressed as monsters
2. Verka Serduchka (Ukraine, 2007) - a comedian dressed as a woman singing about "Dancing Lasha Tumbai"
3. Dschinghis Khan (West Germany, 1979) - a group dressed as Mongolian warriors singing "Dschinghis Khan"
4. Conchita Wurst (Austria, 2014) - a drag queen with a beard singing "Rise Like a Phoenix"
5. Buranovskiye Babushki (Russia, 2012) - a group of elderly singing grandmothers in traditional dress
6. Dustin the Turkey (Ireland, 2008) - a puppet turkey rapping and singing "Irelande Douze Pointe"
7. Silvia Night (Iceland, 2006) - a comedian performing a provocative song and dance routine
8. Guildo Horn (Germany, 1998) - a singer dressed in a sequined suit and angel wings
9. PingPong (Switzerland, 2000) - a band dressed as ping pong balls playing songs about table tennis
10. Scooch (United Kingdom, 2007) - a group dressed as airline stewards singing "Flying the Flag (For You)"
More

11. Cezar (Romania, 2013) - a countertenor wearing a black leather outfit and singing "It's My Life"
12. Genghis Khan (Austria, 1979) - a group dressed as Mongolian warriors singing "Dschinghis Khan"
13. Jedward (Ireland, 2011 and 2012) - a pair of energetic twins with towering blond hair and flashy outfit singing "Lipstick" and "Waterline"
14. Telex (Belgium, 1980) - a band dressed in strange outfits singing "Euro-Vision"
15. Seyran Ohanyan (Armenia, 2009) - a singer performing a song while dressed as a giant tree
16. Hatari (Iceland, 2019) - a BDSM-inspired techno-punk band performing "Hate Will Prevail"
17. Edsilia Rombley (Netherlands, 1998 and 2007) - a singer wearing an enormous red hat in her 1998 performance, and lobsters on her head in 2007
18. Men On A Mission (Sweden, 1999) - a group of bodybuilders dressed in spandex and singing "La La Love"
19. Senit (San Marino, 2011) - a singer performing in a water tank dressed in a mermaid outfit
20. Alla Pugacheva (Russia, 1997) - a diva dressed in an outrageous gown with singing "Primadonna" while standing on a giant seashell.
More

21. Burrito Blanco (Andorra, 2007) - a group dressed in white and playing inflatable dolphins while singing "Casanova"
22. Nina Kraljic (Croatia, 2016) - a singer wearing a stormtrooper-like outfit singing "Lighthouse"
23. Sjonni's Friends (Iceland, 2011) - a group composed of friends of the late Icelandic singer Sjonni Brink, singing in his honor while wearing matching t-shirts and caps
24. Koza Mostra feat. Agathonas Iakovidis (Greece, 2013) - a band playing traditional Greek instruments while a middle-aged man with a mustache sings about alcohol
25. Elitsa & Stoyan (Bulgaria, 2007 and 2013) - a duo playing traditional Bulgarian drums and singing in their native language
26. Roman Lob (Germany, 2012) - a singer performing "Standing Still" while standing in a pool of water
27. Triana Park (Latvia, 2017) - a band with a lead singer wearing a silver jumpsuit and a reflective helmet performing "Line"
28. Imaani (United Kingdom, 1998) - a singer wearing a bright red outfit with a matching hat singing "Where Are You"
29. Jana Burceska (Macedonia, 2017) - a singer performing "Dance Alone" while wearing a wedding dress and being chased by a dancing groom
30. Svetlana Loboda (Ukraine, 2009) - a singer dressed in a gladiator outfit performing "Be My Valentine" with a group of male dancers in leather harnesses.
List of weirdest Eurovision entries, no fakes.

1. Lordi (Finland, 2006) - a heavy metal band dressed as monsters
2. Verka Serduchka (Ukraine, 2007) - a comedian dressed as a woman singing about "Dancing Lasha Tumbai"
3. Genghis Khan (West Germany, 1979) - a group dressed as Mongolian warriors singing "Dschinghis Khan"
4. Conchita Wurst (Austria, 2014) - a drag queen with a beard singing "Rise Like a Phoenix"
5. Buranovskiye Babushki (Russia, 2012) - a group of elderly singing grandmothers in traditional dress
6. Guildo Horn (Germany, 1998) - a singer dressed in a sequined suit and angel wings
7. Cezar (Romania, 2013) - a countertenor wearing a black leather outfit and singing "It's My Life"
8. PingPong (Switzerland, 2000) - a band dressed as ping pong balls playing songs about table tennis
9. Hatari (Iceland, 2019) - a BDSM-inspired techno-punk band performing "Hate Will Prevail"
10. Jedward (Ireland, 2011 and 2012) - a pair of energetic twins with towering blond hair and flashy outfit singing "Lipstick" and "Waterline"
11. Telex (Belgium, 1980) - a band dressed in strange outfits singing "Euro-Vision"
12. Seyran Ohanyan (Armenia, 2009) - a singer performing a song while dressed as a giant tree
13. Edsilia Rombley (Netherlands, 1998 and 2007) - a singer wearing an enormous red hat in her 1998 performance, and lobsters on her head in 2007
14. Men On A Mission (Sweden, 1999) - a group of bodybuilders dressed in spandex and singing "La La Love"
15. Sieneke (Netherlands, 2010) - a young singer in a polka dot dress performing "Ik ben verliefd (Sha-la-lie)"
16. Koza Mostra feat. Agathonas Iakovidis (Greece, 2013) - a band playing traditional Greek instruments while a middle-aged man with a mustache sings about alcohol
17. Freaky Fortune feat. RiskyKidd (Greece, 2014) - a duo dressed in space-themed outfits performing "Rise Up"
18. Triana Park (Latvia, 2017) - a band with a lead singer wearing a silver jumpsuit and a reflective helmet performing "Line"
19. Lithuania (2006) - a group featuring a 17-year-old girl dressed as an angel in a giant wheel
20. Lordi, Verka Serduchka, and Dschinghis Khan (2009) - a medley performance with three of the previously listed artists, performing a mashup of their hits.
List Eurovision acts similar to Dustin the Turkey

1. Silvia Night (Iceland, 2006) - a comedian performing a provocative song and dance routine
2. Latvia (2008) - a group featuring a man with a cow costume singing "Wolves of the Sea"
3. Rodolfo Chikilicuatre (Spain, 2008) - a comedian performing "Baila el Chiki Chiki" while dressed in an outrageous outfit
4. Burrito Blanco (Andorra, 2007) - a group dressed in white and playing inflatable dolphins while singing "Casanova"
5. Aliona Moon (Moldova, 2013) - a singer performing "O mie" while being lifted by a giant dress
6. Elina Nechayeva (Estonia, 2018) - a singer wearing a high-tech dress with digital images that move with the music of "La Forza"
7. Montenegro (2012) - a group consisting of two men and a woman wearing furry animal outfits and singing "Euro Neuro"
8. Rambo Amadeus (Montenegro, 2012) - a singer combining rap, rock, and comedy while wearing a gothic costume
9. Sebalter (Switzerland, 2014) - a singer performing "Hunter of Stars" while playing a violin and surrounded by dancers in traditional Swiss costumes
10. Tijana Bogicevic (Serbia, 2017) - a singer performing "In Too Deep" in a metallic dress and surrounded by dancers in hazmat suits.
List of eurovison acts similar to dancing lasha tumbai

1. Lordi (Finland, 2006)  - a Heavy Metal band dressed as monsters singing "Hard Rock Hallelujah"
2. Verka Serduchka (Ukraine, 2007) - a comedian dressed as a woman singing the non-sensible song "Dancing Lasha Tumbai"
3. Cezar (Romania, 2013) - a countertenor whose performance of "It's My Life" had elements of burlesque and cabaret.
4. Zdob și Zdub (Moldova, 2005) - a band that blends Rock and roll with traditional Moldovan music, their song "Boonika bate doba" had some nonsensical elements to it.
5. Rambo Amadeus (Montenegro, 2012) - a singer and songwriter whose song "Euro Neuro" is meant to be a humorous critique of European society, and the lyrics may seem random and confusing.
6. Koit Toome & Laura (Estonia, 2017) - a duo singing "Verona" with nonsensical lyrics that amused many viewers. 
7. Hatari (Iceland, 2019) - a BDSM-inspired techno-punk band performing "Hate Will Prevail" that may have some non-sensical or confusing elements to it.
8. Carl Espen (Norway, 2014) - a singer performing "Silent Storm" with poetic and metaphorical lyrics that some may find difficult to understand.
9. Lordi, Verka Serduchka and Dschinghis Khan (2009) - a medley performance with three of the previously listed artists, performing a mashup of their hits which includes dancing and unusual elements.
10. Dschinghis Khan (West Germany, 1979) - a group that dressed up as Mongolian warriors and sang the "Dschinghis Khan" which had some nonsensical repeated phrases.
Best eurovison acts before 2000

1. ABBA (Sweden, 1974) - "Waterloo" - ABBA is undoubtedly the most iconic Eurovision winner of all time, and "Waterloo" launched their international success.
2. Celine Dion (Switzerland, 1988) - "Ne Partez Pas Sans Moi" - Celine Dion was already a superstar in Quebec but winning Eurovision helped her achieve global success.
3. Johnny Logan (Ireland, 1980 and 1987) - "What's Another Year" and "Hold Me Now" - Johnny Logan was the first person to win the competition twice as a performer and also won in 1992 as a songwriter.
4. Bucks Fizz (United Kingdom, 1981) - "Making Your Mind Up" - The performance included the famous skirt-ripping choreography and was a huge success in Europe.
5. Brotherhood of Man (United Kingdom, 1976) - "Save Your Kisses for Me" - The song went on to be a number one hit in the UK and the US and has become a classic.
6. Lulu (United Kingdom, 1969) - "Boom Bang-a-Bang" - Lulu's performance captivated audiences and the song became a hit around Europe.
7. Sandie Shaw (United Kingdom, 1967) - "Puppet on a String" - Sandie Shaw's song became a huge hit around Europe and she remains the youngest ever winner of Eurovision at just 20 years old.
8. Dana International (Israel, 1998) - "Diva" - Dana International's win was groundbreaking as the first transgender person ever to win Eurovision.
9. Cliff Richard (United Kingdom, 1968) - "Congratulations" - Although he didn't win, Cliff Richard's performance of "Congratulations" became a classic and was a hit across Europe.
10. France Gall (Luxembourg, 1965) - "Poupée de cire, poupée de son" - France Gall's song was both popular and also marked an important moment in French pop history.
More

11. Gigliola Cinquetti (Italy, 1964) - "Non ho l'età" - At just 16 years old, Gigliola Cinquetti represented Italy with "Non ho l'età" which went on to be a massive hit across Europe.
12. Teach-In (Netherlands, 1975) - "Ding-A-Dong" - Teach-In's song was a cheerful and catchy song that won the hearts of Europe.
13. Vicky Leandros (Luxembourg, 1972) - "Après toi" - Vicky Leandros' song was a big hit in Europe and brought her international fame.
14. Carola (Sweden, 1991) - "Fångad av en stormvind" - Carola's song won the contest for Sweden and became a hit in Scandinavia.
15. Nicole (Germany, 1982) - "Ein bißchen Frieden" - Nicole's song became a big hit in Europe and marked a milestone in German music history.
16. Katrina and the Waves (United Kingdom, 1997) - "Love Shine a Light" - The upbeat song by Katrina and the Waves won Eurovision and has become an anthem of the contest.
17. Lenny Kuhr (Netherlands, 1969) - "De Troubadour" - Lenny Kuhr's heartfelt ballad won the contest for the Netherlands in 1969.
18. Dana (Ireland, 1970) - "All Kinds of Everything" - Dana's win for Ireland marked the country's first victory and the song became a hit around Europe.
19. Anne-Marie David (Luxembourg, 1973; France, 1979) - "Tu te reconnaîtras" (Luxembourg); "Je suis l'enfant soleil" (France) - Anne-Marie David won the contest twice representing different countries and remains a beloved figure in Eurovision history.
20. Toto Cutugno (Italy, 1990) - "Insieme: 1992" - Toto Cutugno's song was a plea for European unity and went on to be a hit in Europe's music charts.
List Eurovision acts similar to Jedward

1. Verka Serduchka (Ukraine, 2007) - a comedian dressed as a woman singing a non-sensical song called "Dancing Lasha Tumbai"
2. Buranovskiye Babushki (Russia, 2012) - a group of elderly singing grandmothers in traditional dress performing "Party for Everybody"
3. Koit Toome & Laura (Estonia, 2017) - a duo singing "Verona" with a bubbly and high-energy performance
4. JOWST feat. Aleksander Walmann (Norway, 2017) - a techno-pop duo with a high-energy performance of "Grab the Moment"
5. Ott Lepland (Estonia, 2012) - a young male singer with a high-energy performance on "Kuula"
6. Tooji (Norway, 2012) - a singer and dancer with a pop-rap fusion song called "Stay"
7. Elnur Huseynov (Azerbaijan, 2015) - a dramatic and theatrical male solo performer with "Hour of the Wolf"
8. Kaliopi (North Macedonia, 2012 and 2016) - a veteran performer with a powerful voice and energetic performances of "Crno i Belo" and "Dona"
9. Aarzemnieki (Latvia, 2014) - a group with a playful and upbeat performance of "Cake to Bake"
10. Les Fatals Picards (France, 2007) - a group with a fun and energetic performance of "L'Amour à la Française"
List weirdest Eurovision semifinalists

1. Hatari (Iceland, 2019) - a BDSM-inspired techno-punk band, known for their controversial performances and avant-garde aesthetic.
2. Lordi (Finland, 2006) - a famously unusual heavy metal band who dressed as monsters and won the Eurovision contest.
3. Verka Serduchka (Ukraine, 2007) - a popular comedian, known for his humorous and non-sensical performance style.
4. Dami Im (Australia, 2016) - a singer with wild hair and a glittered dress, known for her powerhouse vocals and dynamic performances.
5. Conchita Wurst (Austria, 2014) - a drag queen with a beard, known for her powerful vocal performances and message of acceptance.
6. Iveta Mukuchyan (Armenia, 2016) - a singer with a unique fashion sense and a strong voice, performing her song "LoveWave".
7. Jedward (Ireland, 2011 and 2012) - a pair of energetic twins with towering blond hair and flashy outfits, known for their New Kids on the Block-inspired pop songs and dance routines.
8. PKN (Finland, 2015) - a punk rock band, known for their high energy and distinct musical style.
9. Zdob și Zdub (Moldova, 2005) - a band with an eclectic mix of styles including folk, rock, and experimental, and performing their song "Boonika Bate Toba".
10. Tinkara Kovač (Slovenia, 2014) - a flautist and singer with a unique style, performing her song "Round and Round".
